title: The Human Resource Quadrant Behaviour Model (HR-QBM): A Pilot Study on Cognitive-Emotional Thinking Styles and Workplace Satisfaction
author: Sakera, Mandiou
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The Human Resource Quadrant Behaviour Model (HR-QBM) is a novel framework designed to map employee thinking styles along two cognitive–emotional axes (Binary–Complex and Rational–Emotional) and to explore how these patterns relate to workplace satisfaction. This pilot study presents the first empirical application of the HR-QBM using a mixed-methods design with eight participants from a service-sector organization. Quantitative data were collected through Likert-scale items measuring thinking styles and five satisfaction markers (Pay & Recognition, Emotional Support, Growth, Environment, and Purpose). Qualitative insights complemented the numerical quadrant placements. Results indicate consistent and interpretable quadrant profiles, with most participants displaying Complex–Rational tendencies, likely reflecting the young and educationally active convenience sample. Two participants exhibited Emotional-leaning profiles, which aligned closely with their reported workplace challenges. The findings demonstrate preliminary feasibility, conceptual clarity, and practical potential of the HR-QBM for organizational decision-making. Limitations, including sample size and instrument developmental stage, are acknowledged as essential steps toward validating the full HR-QBM framework in future research.
